title: identity.rs: to_secret_bytes_64() returns plain [u8;64] — callers must manually zeroize
description:
Identity::to_secret_bytes_64() (core/src/identity.rs:96) returns [u8; 64] instead of Zeroizing<[u8; 64]>. The export methods secret_bytes() and hpke_secret_bytes() similarly return [u8; 32] by value.

Callers that forget to zeroize leave key material (Ed25519 seed + X25519 seed) on the stack until the frame is reused. Known callers:

- cli/src/commands.rs:151 — does call seed.zeroize() correctly
- core/src/keyfile.rs:104,132 — in tests; seed is never zeroized
- wasm/src/api.rs:23 — passes to B64.encode() and returns; no zeroize
- ffi/src/api/identity.rs:20 — same pattern

The return type of to_secret_bytes_64() should be Zeroizing<[u8; 64]> to enforce zeroization at drop. This is already the pattern used by decrypt_keyfile() (returns Zeroizing<[u8; 64]>).

Note: nie-r9nx.6 covers a different location (hpke.rs internal stack copy). This issue is the public API of Identity itself.
